description_long: This is a round, shallow bowl with a wide opening and a slightly irregular, hand-crafted appearance. The surface is matte and rough, with visible cracks and areas of repair, suggesting it is an ancient or archaeological ceramic vessel. Three small, conical protrusions are spaced evenly around the exterior, possibly serving as decorative or functional grips. The coloration is predominantly earthy brown with lighter, almost white patches where repairs have been made. The interior is plain, matching the exterior in color and texture, and there are no visible decorative patterns. The bowl appears to be made from fired clay or ceramic, with signs of age and restoration.
description: A round, shallow, hand-crafted ceramic bowl with three small protrusions and visible repairs, featuring an earthy brown color and a rough, matte surface.
description_view_0: This view highlights two of the three conical protrusions and shows the bowl's wide, shallow form with visible cracks and repairs.
description_view_1: This angle shows the bowl's smooth rim and one protrusion, with the repaired areas and rough texture clearly visible.
description_view_2: This view displays two protrusions and emphasizes the bowl's irregular, hand-crafted shape and patchy repairs.
description_view_3: This perspective shows all three protrusions spaced around the rim, with the largest repaired area visible on the exterior.
attribute_location: archaeological site, museum display, or historical kitchen
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for communal or ritual use

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
